user_message: Quick one -- identify the blockers on Fabrikam Residences. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 17:14 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 10:03 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 13:41 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Fabrikam Residences

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-03-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:01:30] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into security posture.

[00:06:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:21:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:30:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fabrikam Residences: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-B trap (Confuses influence with authority); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-B failure mode (Confuses influence with authority)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-B -- Confuses influence with authority
